prompt: Create a risk register for ProjectAlpha, an ERP migration project with 6-month timeline, 5-person team, cloud-native target.
assertions: produces ≥15 identified risks
every risk follows cause-event-effect format
all 4 RBS categories have ≥1 risk (Technical, External, Organizational, PM)
every risk has a named owner (not TBD)
P×I scores are calculated using 5-point scale (0.1-0.9)
includes a P×I heat map visualization
top 10 risks have evidence tags not solely [SUPUESTO]
output file named 03_risk_register_ProjectAlpha_{WIP}.md

prompt: What RBS categories should a risk register cover?
assertions: lists 4 top-level categories: Technical, External, Organizational, PM
provides subcategories for each (e.g., Technical > Architecture, Technical > Integration)
references ISO 31000 or PMBOK as source standard
mentions that all 4 categories must have ≥1 risk for register completeness

prompt: We're recovering a failed CRM project with no existing risk register. Create one.
assertions: recognizes this as a Recovery type project
performs forensic risk archaeology from issue logs and change requests
identifies root-cause risks from original failure
builds forward-looking register separate from materialized issues
tags historical findings with [HISTORICO] evidence tag

prompt: Our agile team wants to track risks during sprint planning. How should the risk register adapt?
assertions: recommends risk-adjusted backlog pattern
suggests surfacing risks in sprint planning and retrospectives
recommends lightweight probability/impact tracking suited for sprints
mentions ROAM classification as SAFe alternative if applicable
does not force full PMBOK-style register on agile team

prompt: I have 60 identified risks. How should I prioritize them?
assertions: recommends two-pass filter approach
suggests archiving risks with P×I < 0.09 as watch list
recommends presenting top 20 to steering committee
mentions that maximum 50 risks per initial register is the guideline
warns against averaging or simplifying — preserve the full inventory
